channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Her work brings clean water and new opportunities to tribal communities across the country
item.description: "When we remove some of those barriers to access to safe drinking water, clean drinking water, that we're really freeing up people's time," said Melissa de Vera The post Her work brings clean water and new opportunities to tribal communities across the country first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Sanders: Government should break up OpenAI
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said he believes the government should break up OpenAI, underscoring his concerns about AI's potential impact on the job market and interpersonal relationships.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Why businesses banning AI inevitably lose
item.description: Those AI tools are being trained on our trade secrets. We’ll lose all of our customers if they find out our teams use AI. Our employees will no longer be able to think critically because of the brain rot caused by overreliance on AI. These are not irrational fears.
item.dc:creator: Konstantin Klyagin, Fast Company

item.title: Hackers steal medical records and financial data from 1.2M patients in massive healthcare breach
item.description: A cyberattack on SimonMed Imaging exposed personal information of 1.2 million patients, including medical records, financial details and identity papers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The hot issues for CIOs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025
item.description: The TechCrunch Disrupt conference has long provided valuable insights to and about startups and their investors — but CIOs can learn by talking with startups too, and there’s plenty to learn about at this year’s event.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel sees supply shortage, will prioritize data center technology
item.description: Intel reported better than expected Q3 figures, reflecting a turnaround in sales along with cutting operational expenses and a renewed focus on its core business. On the downside, Intel is enjoying a little too much success and is seeing a shortage of supply.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple may turn off key privacy tool in Europe
item.description: In a victory for surveillance capitalism, Apple may be forced to leave its users in Europe vulnerable to rapacious ad data collection in response to “intense lobbying” by politicians in the region.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Anthropic signs billion-dollar deal with Google Cloud
item.description: US-based AI company Anthropic has signed a major deal with Google Cloud that is said to be worth tens of billions of dollars. As part of the deal, Anthropic will have access to up to one million of Google’s purpose-built Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) AI accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA warns Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager has a worrying security flaw, so patch now
item.description: The critical severity bug is being actively exploited in the wild, possibly by ransomware actors, CISA says.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Spotify gives parents new power to control what their kids hear on streaming platform
item.description: Spotify's managed accounts for kids under 13 now available in at least seven countries, allowing parents to filter and block explicit content and songs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What CIOs can learn at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025
item.description: AI is arguably one of the most disruptive forces for enterprises since the advent of cloud computing, making it a natural focus for many of the presentations at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025. The event, in San Francisco from Oct.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI is connecting all company secrets to ChatGPT - all your work data and apps are now open
item.description: Company knowledge, a self-explanatory ChatGPT tool, will cite internal documents for accurate reporting.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft has allegedly tasked Xbox to deliver a 30% profit margin, and if true, it could explain the reason behind its price hikes and mass layoffs
item.description: Xbox's job cuts and price increases could be due to a push from Microsoft to achieve a 30% profit margin.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, high-profile cryptocurrency figure
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, who created the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange and served prison time for failing to stop criminals from using the platform to move money connected to child sex abuse, drug trafficking and terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Delete the fake VPN app stealing Android users' money
item.description: Cleafy researchers discover fake VPN streaming app Mobdro Pro that installs Klopatra banking Trojan, giving attackers full control over Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Prince Harry, Meghan join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, are joining a call to halt the development of some AI tools until the process can be "done safely and controllably.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Call to ban AI superintelligence could redraw the global tech race between the US and China
item.description: More than 850 prominent figures have called for a prohibition on developing AI superintelligence, a move that could reshape enterprise AI investments and intensify the US-China technology race if adopted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Teens face new PG-13 limits on Instagram
item.description: New Instagram parental controls allow families to manage teen screen time and content limits through the Family Center with stricter safety settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon cloud outage resolved after downing dozens of major websites, apps
item.description: Amazon Web Services provides infrastructure for many big-name companies, as well as state and local governments.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China on Sunday accused the U.S. National Security Agency of carrying out cyberattacks on its national time center, saying any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Discord confirms vendor breach exposed user IDs in ransom plot
item.description: Third-party security breach at Discord exposes sensitive user information including government IDs, highlighting cybersecurity risks from external service providers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
